key_risks: A rebound in mega-cap technology or AI-related stocks would likely make SPY outperform this underweight-tech portfolio.
A weak payrolls or ISM services report could hurt regional banks, industrials, and small value through growth and credit-quality concerns.
Hawkish FOMC minutes or hotter wage data could raise rate expectations and pressure biotech and small-cap valuations.
Recent strength in biotech and regional banks may reverse quickly because some continuation support is price-based and positioning may be extended.

portfolio.rationale: Strong recent relative performance, broad up-day participation, and new 52-week-high proximity suggest near-term demand for domestic financial cyclicals; stable growth data and still-high rates can support bank earnings, though positioning is extended.

Healthcare-linked risk appetite has been strong, with biotech outperforming over the past month and retaining high beta without direct exposure to the AI names that weakened into the entry close; continuation support is mainly price-based, so reversal risk is material.
Healthcare showed strong one-month and one-week relative strength with lower beta than the S&P 500, offering a defensive growth tilt if payrolls or FOMC minutes pressure broader equities.
Industrials have had broad recent participation and may benefit from still-expanding manufacturing data, including June ISM readings above 50 with solid new orders.
Small value combines recent breadth and domestic cyclical exposure with less mega-cap technology concentration than SPY; it could benefit if the market continues to broaden beyond AI leaders.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio tilts away from mega-cap technology after reported AI-related weakness and toward sectors with stronger recent breadth, domestic cyclicality, and healthcare resilience. It aims to outperform SPY over the one-week window if market leadership continues to broaden and upcoming labor/services data are not recessionary.
rationale_summary: Favor regional banks, biotech/healthcare, industrials, and small value over cap-weighted tech concentration. Recent relative strength is used only alongside breadth, macro data, and sector context, with explicit reversal risk acknowledged.
